formalization_percentages.note_kernel_coverage: Over 100% because some items have multiple formalizations
formalization_percentages.note_total: Only 34 of 16,569 total claims have formal equivalents — 0.2%

propositional_formulas.completeness: Low — only the simplest claims are purely propositional

FOL_formulas.quality: MODERATE — predicates are named but not fully defined
FOL_formulas.completeness: Partial — major claims have FOL sketches; full definitions missing

modal_S5_formulas.quality: PARTIAL — modal operators used but S5 frame conditions not verified

deontic_logic_formulas.quality: PARTIAL — O(·), P(·), F(·) operators used but deontic system not specified

ambiguous_percentage.sources: Mysticism definition (CIRC-001), authentic religion (CIRC-005), some derived claims

verdict: The theory is 92% formally expressible at the kernel level — this is strong. However, only 0.2% of the full claim set has been formally expressed. The kernel is the right target for formalization. If the 28 kernel items are fully formalized (target: 100%), the theory achieves formal system status for its core. The 16,569 claims represent philosophical elaboration that does not need full formalization for the core formal system to be valid.
score_rationale: 55/100: kernel near-fully expressible (+) but only sketched (+), not proven in formal calculus (-), 0.2% of total claims (-), deontic/modal logic unspecified (-).
